Patch Set 2:

looks good. does it makes sense to add some protection from a livelock? say OST may have lots of threads running and many sync (due to a reason) operations causing current transaction to close too often. fallocate may get stuck (or make progress too slow) under such an unlucky cicrumstances?
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> looks good. does it makes sense to add some protection from a livelock? say OST may have lots of threads running and many sync (due to a reason) operations causing current transaction to close too often. fallocate may get stuck (or make progress too slow) under such an unlucky cicrumstances?

I think it should have credits to perform at least one iteration of ldiskfs_map_blocks() so it wouldn't be a true livelock. 

but it can be slow b/c fallocate may issue too large allocation requests which have minimum chances to be allocated at C0 and C1 loops.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

I haven't checked if this same  " #ifdef HAVE_LDISKFS_JOURNAL_ENSURE_CREDITS" code is inline somewhere else that might also be cleaned up at the same time.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as f317b5c30e478fdecceea4bd07c85ff305e9d81d by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-16938 utils: setstripe overstripe multiple OST count

commit.subject: LU-17851 ldiskfs: restart long fallocate tx
commit.message:
LU-17851 ldiskfs: restart long fallocate tx

__ext4_journal_ensure_credits() may allow a long fs operation
like fallocate to run for too long, if the initial credits
estimation is enough high.
The fix is to force tx restart if tx state is not T_RUNNING.

HPE-bug-id: LUS-12311
Signed-off-by: Alexander Zarochentsev <alexander.zarochentsev@hpe.com>
Change-Id: Ib03d78739997caa6d13690b41ef7d01609a3623b
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55111
Reviewed-by: Andrew Perepechko <andrew.perepechko@hpe.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
